120:     TVal,
121:     TId
122:   > {
123:     if (!isAnyOf(this.config.type, ["option", "multiOption"])) {
124:       throw new Error(
125:         "transformOptionFn() is only applicable to option or multiOption columns",
126:       );
127:     }
128:     const newInstance = this.clone() as any;
129:     newInstance.config.transformOptionFn = fn;
130:     return newInstance;
131:   }
132: 
133:   orderFn(
134:     fn: TOrderFn<TVal>,
135:   ): ColumnConfigBuilder<
136:     TData,
137:     TType extends "option" | "multiOption" ? TType : never,
138:     TVal,
139:     TId
140:   > {
141:     if (!isAnyOf(this.config.type, ["option", "multiOption"])) {
142:       throw new Error(
143:         "orderFn() is only applicable to option or multiOption columns",
144:       );
145:     }
146:     const newInstance = this.clone() as any;
147:     newInstance.config.orderFn = fn;
148:     return newInstance;
149:   }
150: 
151:   build(): ColumnConfig<TData, TType, TVal, TId> {
152:     if (!this.config.id) throw new Error("id is required");
153:     if (!this.config.accessor) throw new Error("accessor is required");
154:     if (!this.config.displayName) throw new Error("displayName is required");
155:     if (!this.config.icon) throw new Error("icon is required");
156:     return this.config as ColumnConfig<TData, TType, TVal, TId>;
157:   }
158: }
159: 
160: // Update the helper interface
161: interface FluentColumnConfigHelper<TData> {
162:   text: () => ColumnConfigBuilder<TData, "text", string>;
163:   number: () => ColumnConfigBuilder<TData, "number", number>;
164:   date: () => ColumnConfigBuilder<TData, "date", Date>;
165:   option: () => ColumnConfigBuilder<TData, "option", string>;
166:   multiOption: () => ColumnConfigBuilder<TData, "multiOption", string[]>;
167: }
168: 
169: // Factory function remains mostly the same
170: export function createColumnConfigHelper<
171:   TData,
172: >(): FluentColumnConfigHelper<TData> {
173:   return {
174:     date: () => new ColumnConfigBuilder<TData, "date", Date>("date"),
175:     multiOption: () =>
176:       new ColumnConfigBuilder<TData, "multiOption", string[]>("multiOption"),
177:     number: () => new ColumnConfigBuilder<TData, "number", number>("number"),
178:     option: () => new ColumnConfigBuilder<TData, "option", string>("option"),
179:     text: () => new ColumnConfigBuilder<TData, "text", string>("text"),
180:   };
181: }
182: 
183: export function getColumnOptions<TData, TType extends ColumnDataType, TVal>(
184:   column: ColumnConfig<TData, TType, TVal>,
185:   data: TData[],
186:   strategy: FilterStrategy,
187: ): ColumnOption[] {
188:   if (!isAnyOf(column.type, ["option", "multiOption"])) {
189:     console.warn(
190:       "Column options can only be retrieved for option and multiOption columns",
191:     );
192:     return [];
193:   }
194: 
195:   if (strategy === "server" && !column.options) {
196:     throw new Error("column options are required for server-side filtering");
197:   }
198: 
199:   if (column.options) {
200:     return column.options;
201:   }
202: 
203:   const filtered = data
204:     .flatMap(column.accessor)
205:     .filter((v): v is NonNullable<TVal> => v !== undefined && v !== null);
206: 
207:   let models = uniq(filtered);
208: 
209:   if (column.orderFn) {
210:     models = models.sort((m1, m2) =>
211:       column.orderFn!(
212:         m1 as ElementType<NonNullable<TVal>>,
213:         m2 as ElementType<NonNullable<TVal>>,
214:       ),
215:     );
216:   }
217: 
218:   if (column.transformOptionFn) {
219:     // Memoize transformOptionFn calls
220:     const memoizedTransform = memo(
221:       () => [models],
222:       (deps) =>
223:         deps[0].map((m) =>
224:           column.transformOptionFn!(m as ElementType<NonNullable<TVal>>),
225:         ),
226:       { key: `transform-${column.id}` },
227:     );
228:     return memoizedTransform();
229:   }
230: 
231:   if (isColumnOptionArray(models)) return models;
232: 
233:   throw new Error(
234:     `[data-table-filter] [${column.id}] Either provide static options, a transformOptionFn, or ensure the column data conforms to ColumnOption type`,
235:   );
236: }
237: 
238: export function getColumnValues<TData, TType extends ColumnDataType, TVal>(
239:   column: ColumnConfig<TData, TType, TVal>,
240:   data: TData[],
241: ) {
242:   // Memoize accessor calls
243:   const memoizedAccessor = memo(
244:     () => [data],
245:     (deps) =>
246:       deps[0]
247:         .flatMap(column.accessor)
248:         .filter(
249:           (v): v is NonNullable<TVal> => v !== undefined && v !== null,
250:         ) as ElementType<NonNullable<TVal>>[],
251:     { key: `accessor-${column.id}` },
252:   );
253: 
254:   const raw = memoizedAccessor();
255: 
256:   if (!isAnyOf(column.type, ["option", "multiOption"])) {
257:     return raw;
258:   }
259: 
260:   if (column.options) {
261:     return raw
262:       .map((v) => column.options?.find((o) => o.value === v)?.value)
263:       .filter((v) => v !== undefined && v !== null);
264:   }
265: 
266:   if (column.transformOptionFn) {
267:     const memoizedTransform = memo(
268:       () => [raw],
269:       (deps) =>
270:         deps[0].map(
271:           (v) => column.transformOptionFn!(v) as ElementType<NonNullable<TVal>>,
272:         ),
273:       { key: `transform-values-${column.id}` },
274:     );
275:     return memoizedTransform();
276:   }
277: 
278:   if (isColumnOptionArray(raw)) {
279:     return raw;
280:   }
281: 
282:   throw new Error(
283:     `[data-table-filter] [${column.id}] Either provide static options, a transformOptionFn, or ensure the column data conforms to ColumnOption type`,
284:   );
285: }
286: 
287: export function getFacetedUniqueValues<
288:   TData,
289:   TType extends ColumnDataType,
290:   TVal,
291: >(
292:   column: ColumnConfig<TData, TType, TVal>,
293:   values: string[] | ColumnOption[],
294:   strategy: FilterStrategy,
295: ): Map<string, number> | undefined {
296:   if (!isAnyOf(column.type, ["option", "multiOption"])) {
297:     console.warn(
298:       "Faceted unique values can only be retrieved for option and multiOption columns",
299:     );
300:     return new Map<string, number>();
301:   }
302: 
303:   if (strategy === "server") {
304:     return column.facetedOptions;
305:   }
306: 
307:   const acc = new Map<string, number>();
308: 
309:   if (isColumnOptionArray(values)) {
310:     for (const option of values) {
311:       const curr = acc.get(option.value) ?? 0;
312:       acc.set(option.value, curr + 1);
313:     }
314:   } else {
315:     for (const option of values) {
316:       const curr = acc.get(option as string) ?? 0;
317:       acc.set(option as string, curr + 1);
318:     }
319:   }
320: 
321:   return acc;
322: }
323: 
324: export function getFacetedMinMaxValues<
325:   TData,
326:   TType extends ColumnDataType,
327:   TVal,
328: >(
329:   column: ColumnConfig<TData, TType, TVal>,
330:   data: TData[],
331:   strategy: FilterStrategy,
332: ): [number, number] | undefined {
333:   if (column.type !== "number") return undefined; // Only applicable to number columns
334: 
335:   if (typeof column.min === "number" && typeof column.max === "number") {
336:     return [column.min, column.max];
337:   }
338: 
339:   if (strategy === "server") {
340:     return undefined;
341:   }
342: 
343:   const values = data
344:     .flatMap((row) => column.accessor(row) as Nullable<number>)
345:     .filter((v): v is number => typeof v === "number" && !Number.isNaN(v));
346: 
347:   if (values.length === 0) {
348:     return [0, 0]; // Fallback to config or reasonable defaults
349:   }
350: 
351:   const min = Math.min(...values);
352:   const max = Math.max(...values);
353: 
354:   return [min, max];
355: }
356: 
357: export function createColumns<TData>(
358:   data: TData[],
359:   columnConfigs: ReadonlyArray<ColumnConfig<TData, any, any, any>>,
360:   strategy: FilterStrategy,
361: ): Column<TData>[] {
362:   return columnConfigs.map((columnConfig) => {
363:     const getOptions: () => ColumnOption[] = memo(
364:       () => [data, strategy, columnConfig.options],
365:       ([data, strategy]) =>
366:         getColumnOptions(columnConfig, data as any, strategy as any),
367:       { key: `options-${columnConfig.id}` },
368:     );
369: 
370:     const getValues: () => ElementType<NonNullable<any>>[] = memo(
371:       () => [data, strategy],
372:       () => (strategy === "client" ? getColumnValues(columnConfig, data) : []),
373:       { key: `values-${columnConfig.id}` },
374:     );
375: 
376:     const getUniqueValues: () => Map<string, number> | undefined = memo(
377:       () => [getValues(), strategy],
378:       ([values, strategy]) =>
379:         getFacetedUniqueValues(columnConfig, values as any, strategy as any),
380:       { key: `faceted-${columnConfig.id}` },
381:     );
382: 
383:     const getMinMaxValues: () => [number, number] | undefined = memo(
384:       () => [data, strategy],
385:       () => getFacetedMinMaxValues(columnConfig, data, strategy),
386:       { key: `minmax-${columnConfig.id}` },
387:     );
388: 
389:     // Create the Column instance
390:     const column: Column<TData> = {
391:       ...columnConfig,
392:       _prefetchedFacetedMinMaxValuesCache: null,
393:       _prefetchedFacetedUniqueValuesCache: null,
394:       _prefetchedOptionsCache: null, // Initialize private cache
395:       _prefetchedValuesCache: null,
396:       getFacetedMinMaxValues: getMinMaxValues,
397:       getFacetedUniqueValues: getUniqueValues,
398:       getOptions,
399:       getValues,
400:       prefetchFacetedMinMaxValues: async () => {},
401:       prefetchFacetedUniqueValues: async () => {},
402:       // Prefetch methods will be added below
403:       prefetchOptions: async () => {}, // Placeholder, defined below
404:       prefetchValues: async () => {},
405:     };
406: 
407:     if (strategy === "client") {
408:       // Define prefetch methods with access to the column instance
409:       column.prefetchOptions = async (): Promise<void> => {
410:         if (!column._prefetchedOptionsCache) {
411:           await new Promise((resolve) =>
412:             setTimeout(() => {
413:               const options = getOptions();
414:               column._prefetchedOptionsCache = options;
415:               // console.log(`Prefetched options for ${columnConfig.id}`)
416:               resolve(undefined);
417:             }, 0),
418:           );
419:         }
420:       };
421: 
422:       column.prefetchValues = async (): Promise<void> => {
423:         if (!column._prefetchedValuesCache) {
424:           await new Promise((resolve) =>
425:             setTimeout(() => {
426:               const values = getValues();
427:               column._prefetchedValuesCache = values;
428:               // console.log(`Prefetched values for ${columnConfig.id}`)
429:               resolve(undefined);
430:             }, 0),
431:           );
432:         }
433:       };
434: 
435:       column.prefetchFacetedUniqueValues = async (): Promise<void> => {
436:         if (!column._prefetchedFacetedUniqueValuesCache) {
437:           await new Promise((resolve) =>
438:             setTimeout(() => {
439:               const facetedMap = getUniqueValues();
440:               column._prefetchedFacetedUniqueValuesCache = facetedMap ?? null;
441:               // console.log(
442:               //   `Prefetched faceted unique values for ${columnConfig.id}`,
443:               // )
444:               resolve(undefined);
445:             }, 0),
446:           );
447:         }
448:       };
449: 
450:       column.prefetchFacetedMinMaxValues = async (): Promise<void> => {
451:         if (!column._prefetchedFacetedMinMaxValuesCache) {
452:           await new Promise((resolve) =>
453:             setTimeout(() => {
454:               const value = getMinMaxValues();
455:               column._prefetchedFacetedMinMaxValuesCache = value ?? null;
456:               // console.log(
457:               //   `Prefetched faceted min/max values for ${columnConfig.id}`,
458:               // )
459:               resolve(undefined);
460:             }, 0),
461:           );
462:         }
463:       };
464:     }
465: 
466:     return column;
467:   });
468: }

380: export function determineNewOperator<TType extends ColumnDataType>(
381:   type: TType,

383:   nextVals: FilterValues<TType>,

388:       ? oldVals[0].length
389:       : oldVals.length;
390:   const b =
391:     Array.isArray(nextVals) && Array.isArray(nextVals[0])
392:       ? nextVals[0].length
393:       : nextVals.length;
394: 
395:   // If filter size has not transitioned from single to multiple (or vice versa)
396:   // or is unchanged, return the current operator.
397:   if (a === b || (a >= 2 && b >= 2) || (a <= 1 && b <= 1))
398:     return currentOperator;
399: 
400:   const opDetails = filterTypeOperatorDetails[type][currentOperator];
401: 
402:   // Handle transition from single to multiple filter values.
403:   if (a < b && b >= 2) return opDetails.singularOf ?? currentOperator;
404:   // Handle transition from multiple to single filter values.
405:   if (a > b && b <= 1) return opDetails.pluralOf ?? currentOperator;
406:   return currentOperator;

Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Here's my plan:

---

## Plan: Filter Combobox Input

### Goal
Replace the current separate `<Input>` (search) + `<DataTableFilter>` (filter button/popover) + `<ActiveFilters>` (filter pills) layout with a single **combobox-like input component** that:
1. Shows applied filters as inline pills/chips **inside** the input
2. Allows keyboard navigation between pills (left/right arrow to move between, Backspace to remove selected)
3. Typing opens a command-menu popover to add new filters (reusing existing `FilterSelector`/`FilterValueController` logic)
4. Supports free-text search alongside filters

### Architecture

#### New Component: `FilterCombobox`
**File:** `src/components/data-table-filter/components/filter-combobox.tsx`

A compound input that merges the search input + filter pills + filter-add popover into one unified field.

**Structure:**
```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 🔍  [Status ● Active ×] [Assignee ● John ×]  |  type…  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
         ↑ filter pills (inline)     ↑ text cursor
```

When the user types, a command popover drops down showing:
- Column names to filter on (same as current `FilterSelector` step 1)
- Quick search results for option/multiOption columns (same as `QuickSearchFilters`)

**Props:** Same data as current `DataTableFilter` + search value/onChange + placeholder

#### Key Implementation Details

1. **Internal layout:** A `<div>` styled like the existing `<Input>` (same border, height, rounded-lg, focus ring) containing:
   - Search icon (left, absolute)
   - Flex row of filter pill elements + a bare `<input>` at the end
   - Each pill is a mini `ActiveFilter` (icon + operator + value + × button), but compact (smaller, no rounded-2xl — more badge-like)

2. **Keyboard behavior:**
   - **ArrowLeft** when cursor at position 0: select/move to previous pill
   - **ArrowRight** on a selected pill: deselect and move cursor into input (or to next pill)
   - **Backspace** on empty input or on a selected pill: remove the filter
   - **Enter** on a selected pill: open that filter's value editor popover
   - **Typing** when a pill is selected: deselect pill, start typing
   - **Escape**: close popover / deselect pill

3. **Popover for adding filters:** Reuses the existing `Command`/`CommandInput`/`CommandItem` pattern from `filter-selector.tsx` — same two-step flow (pick column → pick value). The popover is anchored to the input container.

4. **Filter pills:** A simplified inline version of `ActiveFilter` — just `icon operator value ×` — no nested popovers for editing operator/value directly. Instead, clicking a pill opens its value editor (same as current behavior, but triggered from the pill inside the input).

### Files to Create/Modify

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `src/components/data-table-filter/components/filter-combobox.tsx` | **CREATE** — Main combobox input component |
| `src/components/data-table-filter/components/filter-pill.tsx` | **CREATE** — Compact filter pill (inline badge with icon, operator label, value display, remove button) |
| `src/components/data-table-filter/index.tsx` | **MODIFY** — Export `FilterCombobox` |
| `src/components/layouts/data-view-layout.tsx` | **MODIFY** — Replace the `<Input>` + `<DataTableFilter>` + `<FilterActions>` in the header with a single `<FilterCombobox>` |

### Key Design Decisions

1. **Reuse existing popover/command infrastructure** — The filter-add flow uses the same `Command` components and `FilterValueController` from the existing filter-selector. No need to rebuild the filter value selection logic.

2. **Filter pills are read-only inline** — Unlike the current `ActiveFilter` which has separate popovers for operator and value, the inline pill shows a compact label and clicking it opens a single popover for that filter (combining operator + value editing). This keeps the input clean.

3. **Search is preserved** — The text typed in the input still drives the `search` state (text search). The command popover for adding filters is a separate concern triggered by the input state.

4. **No card design** — Following the project's design rules, the component is a flat input field with inline badges, no card containers.

5. **"Clear all" button** — Appears inside the input when filters are present (right side, before the input end), similar to a clearable search input pattern.

All done. Here's a summary of what was built:

### New Files
- **`src/components/data-table-filter/components/filter-pill.tsx`** — Compact inline filter badge with icon, operator, value, and remove button. Clicking opens a popover to edit the filter's operator and value (using existing `FilterOperator` + `FilterValueController`).

- **`src/components/data-table-filter/components/filter-combobox.tsx`** — Combobox-like input that merges search + filter pills + add-filter popover into one unified field:
  - Filter pills rendered inline before the text input
  - Typing opens a command popover to add new filters (column selection → value controller)
  - `ArrowLeft` at cursor start navigates to previous pill
  - `ArrowRight` on selected pill navigates to next or into input
  - `Backspace` on empty input or selected pill removes the filter
  - `Enter` on selected pill opens its edit popover
  - `Escape` closes popover / deselects pill
  - Clear-all button (`FilterXIcon`) when filters exist

### Modified Files
- **`src/components/data-table-filter/index.tsx`** — Added `FilterCombobox` export
- **`src/components/layouts/data-view-layout.tsx`** — Replaced `<Input>` + `<DataTableFilter>` with `<FilterCombobox>`, removed unused `Search` import
